start_cue: Fit beats brand every time. A $20 shirt that fits your shoulders outperforms a $300 piece that doesn't. Audit before you buy.

weeks.focus: Fit audit — keep what fits, remove what doesn't
weeks.detail: Try on every shirt, pant, and jacket you currently wear. Keep what meets the fit checks: shoulder seam ends at your actual shoulder point, sleeves end at the wrist bone, shirts don't billow at the waist, pants taper without clinging. Donate or retire the rest — keeping poorly-fitting items crowds the closet with ways to look worse. For borderline items, a local tailor can fix sleeves, hems, and waist suppression cheaply ($15-30 per alteration).

weeks.focus: Build the simple wardrobe in core colors
weeks.detail: The repeatable-winner list: fitted plain tees in black, white, grey, navy; dark and light wash jeans; well-fitted chinos; minimal sneakers (one white pair, one black or dark); one versatile jacket (denim, bomber, or leather, picked for your vibe). This covers ~80% of real occasions. Neutral colors near the face, avoid horizontal-stripe patterns that read as wider, nothing flashy until the baseline is running.

weeks.focus: Pick an archetype and build consistency
weeks.detail: Choose one base style and stick with it: clean minimalist (neutral, sharp fits, effortless), athletic casual (fitted, performance-inspired, subtly shows physique), or rugged (denim, boots, heavier textures). The archetype should match your face and build — softer face does poorly in aggressive styles, bulkier build needs structure over tightness. Consistency within one archetype creates identity; mixing archetypes reads as disconnected.

Learn to dress to the room — overdressed reads as insecure, consistently underdressed reads as careless. Keep shoes clean; scuffed or beat-up shoes undermine the entire outfit regardless of what's above them. One good watch (see the niche-enhancements walkthrough if you have that goal). Color accents near the upper body only — lower-half color is usually noise.

graduation: When fit is reliable, the core palette works without thinking, an archetype has settled in, and you can dress for any common occasion without a second thought, the system is installed. Further upgrades become about quality (one excellent piece every few months) rather than quantity or trend-chasing.
